title: Creating New EC2 Instance using Snapshot

tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.

On the AWS sign-in page, Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab. Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to Services menu in the top, then click on EC2 in the Compute section.
Click on Instances from the left side bar and then click on Launch Instances
Name : Enter MyEC2Server
Task 2 : Launch an EC2 Instance
For Amazon Machine Image (AMI): Search for Amazon Linux 2023 kernel-6.1 AMI in the search box and click on the Select button.
Task 2 : Launch an EC2 Instance
For Instance Type: select t2.micro
Task 2 : Launch an EC2 Instance
For Key pair: Select Create a new key pair Button Key pair name: WhizKey Key pair type: RSA Private key file format: .pem
For Key pair: Select Create a new key pair Button
Key pair name: WhizKey
Key pair type: RSA
Private key file format: .pem
Select Create key pair Button.
In Network Settings Click on Edit:
Auto-assign public IP: Enable
Select Create new Security group
Security group name : Enter MyEC2Server_SG
Description : Enter Security Group to allow traffic to EC2
Task 2 : Launch an EC2 Instance
Check Allow SSH from and Select Anywhere from dropdown
To add SSH, Choose Type: SSH Source: Select Anywhere
To add SSH,
Choose Type: SSH
Source: Select Anywhere
For HTTP, Click on Add security group rule Choose Type: HTTP Source: Select Anywhere
For HTTP, Click on Add security group rule
Choose Type: HTTP
Source: Select Anywhere
Click on Advanced Details and under the User data: section, enter the following script to create an HTML page served by an Apache httpd web server.
Keep Rest thing Default and Click on Launch Instance Button.
Select View all Instances to View Instance you Created

Launch Status: Your instances are now launching, Navigate to Instances page from left menu and wait the status of the EC2 Instance changes to running and health check status changes to 2/2 checks passed.
Task 2 : Launch an EC2 Instance
Select the Instance and Copy the Public IPv4 Address
To test if the HTML page published, copy the Public IP address and run it on the browser.
Task 2 : Launch an EC2 Instance

tasks.title: Task 3 : Create a Snapshot of EC2 Instance

steps.text: Navigate to Snapshots section under Elastic Block Store from the left menu.
Click on Create Snapshot button.
In the Create snapshot page, fill the details below.
Resource type: Select Instance
Instance ID: Select MyEC2Server from the drop-down menu.
Description : Enter MyEC2Snapshot
Encrypted: Leave as it is.
Tags Click on Add Tags button Key : Enter Name Value : Enter MyEC2Snapshot
Tags Click on Add Tags button
Key : Enter Name
Value : Enter MyEC2Snapshot
Task 3 : Create a Snapshot of EC2 Instance
Once the snapshot creation is completed, the status will change to completed.
Task 3 : Create a Snapshot of EC2 Instance

tasks.title: Task 4 : Create an AMI from the Snapshot

steps.text: Select the Snapshot that you have created.
Click on the Action button on top right menu and select Create image from snapshot.
Task 4 : Create an AMI from the Snapshot

A pop-up window will show up. Provide the details as mentioned below to configure your AMI: Name: Enter MyEC2AMI Description: Enter My EC2 AMI Leave other settings as default. Click on Create Image button.
A pop-up window will show up. Provide the details as mentioned below to configure your AMI:
Name: Enter MyEC2AMI
Description: Enter My EC2 AMI
Leave other settings as default.
Click on Create Image button.
Navigate to AMIs under IMAGES in the left menu to see the newly created AMI.
Task 4 : Create an AMI from the Snapshot

tasks.title: Task 5 : Launch an EC2 Instance using newly created AMI

steps.text: Select the AMI that you have created and click on the Launch Instance from AMI button to launch a new instance.
Task 5 : Launch an EC2 Instance using newly created AMI
Name : Enter MyEC2AMIServer
Task 5 : Launch an EC2 Instance using newly created AMI
For Instance Type: select t2.micro
Task 5 : Launch an EC2 Instance using newly created AMI
For Key pair: Select the key you created previously.
In Network Settings :
Auto-assign public IP: Enable
Select Select existing security group and Select the group created earlier
Click on Advanced Details and under the User data: section, enter the following script to create an HTML page served by an Apache httpd web server.
Note: After pasting the user data, make sure to remove extra spacing.
Keep Rest thing Default and Click on Launch Instance Button.
Select View all Instances to View Instance you Created
Launch Status: Your instances are now launching, Navigate to Instances page from left menu. Wait the status of the EC2 Instance changes to running and health check status changes to 2/2 checks passed
Task 5 : Launch an EC2 Instance using newly created AMI

tasks.title: Task 6 : Test the new EC2 instance created from AMI

steps.text: Once your new instance status is running, copy its Public IP.
Task 6 : Test the new EC2 instance created from AMI
Paste it into your browser.
You will be able to see the output of MyEC2AMIServer is the same as the one we got from the initial EC2 instance MyEC2Server.
Task 6 : Test the new EC2 instance created from AMI
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the right side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample output :
Task 7 : Validation of the lab
You have successfully launched an EC2 Instance and created a Snapshot from it.
You have successfully created an AMI from the Snapshot.
You have successfully launched a new EC2 instance from the AMI and tested it.
Sign out of AWS Account.
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps, click on End Lab from your lab lab console and wait till the process gets completed.
